channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: The US, Israel and the normalisation of scandal
item.description: Thanks to Netanyahu and Trump, scandal is now our new normal - call it 'Everythinggate'.

item.title: Khartoum’s slow recovery amid cautious return
item.description: War-damaged Khartoum sees uneven recovery as residents weigh return amid fragile services and economy.

item.title: Starbucks Korea CEO fired over promotion that evoked military crackdown
item.description: 'Tank Day' marketing campaign provoked backlash for eliciting bloody suppression of 1980 Gwangju uprising.

item.title: ‘China holds the cards’: Why Putin’s visit to Beijing after Trump matters
item.description: Scant progress in US-China talks bolsters Putin, while for China, hosting back-to-back visits is a diplomatic flex.

item.title: Child survivor of San Diego mosque shooting describes ordeal
item.description: A boy who survived the San Diego mosque shooting that killed three people has described seeing ‘bad stuff’.

item.title: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China
item.description: The affected area extends more than 1,000km (620 miles), according to Chinese meteorologists.

item.title: ‘King of the North’: Who is Andy Burnham, a potential UK prime minister?
item.description: With Keir Starmer under fire, it is Manchester’s mayor who looks to be the key challenger.

item.title: San Diego mosque shooting: What we know about the victims and the attackers
item.description: Authorities said the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego is being investigated as a possible hate crime.

item.title: Gunmen abduct 39 students, 7 teachers in attacks on Nigeria schools
item.description: The attacks targeted a secondary school and two primary schools, as gunmen kidnapped children aged between two and 16.

item.title: Lebanon and Syria reshape ties amid Israeli attacks and regional shifts
item.description: Damascus and Beirut focus on sovereignty and cooperation, moving away from decades of Syrian dominance over Lebanon.

item.title: Los Angeles World Cup stadium workers threaten strike over ICE deployment
item.description: Workers represented by a local union say ICE presence would create a climate of fear during the FIFA World Cup 2026.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Jerusalem Day, Nakba mark week of surging attacks
item.description: Far-right marchers in the Old City chant "death to Arabs" while Israel kills Hamas military chief in Gaza.

item.title: Russian strike damages Ukraine Danube port as Moscow intercepts drones
item.description: Port infrastructure hit in southern Ukraine as drones intercepted over Russian capital.

item.title: Iran war day 81: Trump delays attack; Tehran says won’t ‘surrender’
item.description: US president says he held off a scheduled attack on Iran after pressure from Gulf allies as renewed talks gain momentum.

item.title: India-Nordic summit: Why is Modi wooing Northern Europe?
item.description: Technology, green transition, geopolitics and India's role in the Arctic region are among key topics on the agenda.

item.title: Wembanyama scores 41 as Spurs upset Thunder in Game 1 thriller
item.description: French centre dominates as San Antonio steal a crucial 122–115 road win against defending NBA champions.

item.title: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding president resign
item.description: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding that President Paz resign.

item.title: Helicopters douse flames threatening hillside homes in California
item.description: Video shows helicopters dumping water on a fast-moving wildfire in southern California’s Simi Valley.

item.title: How Philadelphia’s Democratic primary tests the bounds of US progressivism
item.description: The race for Pennsylvania's third congressional district reveals divisions between Democrats closely aligned on policy.

item.title: Trump’s repeated ultimatums betray his lack of leverage over Iran
item.description: Foad Izadi says US President Trump's repeated threats of renewed full-scale war are a sign of weakness.

item.title: WHO responding to ‘complex, difficult’ Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization is getting health experts and supplies to DR Congo to battle the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Russia-China ties ‘stabilising’ force in world, Putin says before Xi talks
item.description: Russia's Vladimir Putin is travelling to China for second meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in less than a year.

item.title: Two teen gunmen kill three at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime
item.description: Two teen gunmen kill three at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump says Iran attack postponed at request of Gulf allies
item.description: Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran says Iran's leaders are 'projecting defiance' and rejecting 'pressure' from the US.

item.title: US suspends joint defence effort with Canada dating back to World War II
item.description: The Trump administration has frequently accused US allies of failing to live up to mutual defence obligations.
